title: Your Money or Your Life
subtitle: 9 Steps to Transforming Your Relationship with Money and Achieving Financial Independence

authors.name: Vicki Robin
authors.role: Author, speaker, and co-founder of the New Road Map Foundation. Graduated with honors from Brown University. Left a career in film and theater to develop and teach the 9-step program.

authors.name: Joe Dominguez
authors.role: Former Wall Street financial analyst who retired at age 31. Created the original 9-step program. Co-founder of the New Road Map Foundation. His ultra-conservative investment approach favored US Treasury bonds over equities.

themes: Money is life energy — time spent working, not a separate abstraction
More money does not equal more happiness beyond the point of enough
Financial independence means your investment income covers your expenses
Conscious spending aligned with values brings fulfillment
The work-and-spend treadmill is both personally and environmentally destructive
Frugality is not deprivation — it is enjoying full value from every unit of life energy
Tracking every cent creates awareness that transforms behavior
awards: Founding text of the FIRE (Financial Independence Retire Early) movement
excerpt: Money is something we trade our life energy for — but we rarely stop to ask whether the trade is a good one. This book offers nine steps to transform your relationship with money, so you can stop making a dying and start making a living.
